description: Native English (American). Male, mid-30s. High-quality recording. Persona: weathered working father. Emotion: plain, weary, steady. A quiet, clipped Detroit working-class voice, lightly weathered but not aged, a flat baritone with words spaced and economical. He answers with the thing itself, ends sentences early, softening only at the edges.
text: [sighs] Door's gone quiet again. I'll run it to Eli... he'll make it ring, won't take a dime off me. That's all we want. [tired] A house that knows somebody's in it. The boy hearing someone came.
